content:
# Structural Intelligence P3: Barrel File and __init__.py Resolution -- Decision Record (2026-03-29)
## What: Improved wire_pipeline_relations() target resolution with barrel file and __init__.py fallbacks.
## Key Design Decisions:
1. HashMap file index: built once per wire_pipeline_relations() call, converts target file lookup from O(n) to O(1). Uses &str keys borrowing from processed slice (zero-copy).
2. Fallback chain via or_else: direct match -> JS/TS barrel (index.ts/tsx/js/jsx) -> Python __init__.py. Each stage only fires on miss. Existing Rust CRATE_REF resolution unaffected.
3. Extension probe order: .ts -> .tsx -> .js -> .jsx matching TypeScript's own module resolver priority.
4. Python init.py: converts dots to slashes (package.submodule -> package/submodule/__init__.py). Runs unconditionally on miss (low cost: one allocation + one HashMap miss for non-Python paths).
5. Extracted resolve_target() helper for reuse across all three resolution strategies.
## Review Findings Worth Remembering:
- Source-side from_idx lookup is still O(n) linear scan. A composite key HashMap (source_file, start_line) -> idx would complete the optimization. Follow-up item.
- Barrel and __init__.py fallbacks fire unconditionally for all languages. Low practical risk since phantom paths must exist in file_index to produce false matches. Could gate on heuristics in future.
## What Blocked / Surprised:
- Nothing blocked. The refactoring was clean and all 5 reviewers approved without blocking issues.
